text: Managing a team effectively requires several key skills. First, clear communication is essential. You must explain tasks and goals in a way everyone understands. Delegation is also important; assign work based on each person's strengths rather than doing everything yourself. Provide regular feedback, both positive and constructive, to help team members improve. To keep motivation high, recognize effort and celebrate small wins. Finally, conflict resolution is crucial. When disagreements arise, listen to all sides and guide the team toward a fair solution. A manager who balances these elements can build a productive and harmonious team.
